new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a spirit that qualifies as vodka as defined in HS heading 2208 explanatory note paragraph 3, item 5: a colourless, odourless, tasteless, and neutral spirit produced by distillation or rectification of agricultural origin ethyl alcohol, typically from grain or potato, at a high alcoholic strength.

The distillation or rectification process used to produce vodka from agricultural origin ethyl alcohol (typically grain or potato), yielding a neutral, colourless spirit, as described in HS heading 2208 explanatory note paragraph 3, item 5.

new_classes.label_de: Wodka, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 l (KN 2208 60 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Vodka as described in HS heading 2208 explanatory note paragraph 3, item 5, presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres. Corresponds to CN subheading 2208 60 11.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for 22086011 states: "Wodka – Siehe die Erläuterungen zu Position 2208 des HS, dritter Absatz, Ziffer 5." The key discriminating criteria captured are: (1) the product is vodka (WodkaQuality), (2) produced by distillation/rectification from agricultural ethyl alcohol (WodkaDistillationProcess), and (3) presented in containers ≤ 2 l (SmallContainer). The sibling code 22086019 covers vodka in containers > 2 l, so the container size restriction is essential for discrimination. The HS explanatory note content (neutral, colourless, odourless/tasteless spirit from grain/potato) is captured qualitatively via WodkaQuality but cannot be fully formalised as OWL datatype restrictions without additional properties (e.g. colour, aroma, taste properties) not present in the TBox. Coverage is therefore 0.85 rather than 1.0 due to the inability to fully formalise the organoleptic neutrality criteria from the HS explanatory note.
